title: [Epic] Foundation: free-only distributed counter platform scaffold
body:
Objective: establish the free-only repo, architecture boundaries, and first shippable vertical slice.

Acceptance criteria:
- Service layout and tooling are locked.
- Increment -> stream -> worker -> Redis -> read path works.
- Basic metrics/health endpoints are operational.

title: [Feature] Scaffold Java 21 + Spring Boot service layout
body: Create Java 21 + Spring Boot multi-module layout for gateway and worker services. Add Maven wrapper, baseline build/test scripts, and local run entrypoints compatible with compose.

title: [Feature] Implement POST /counters/{id}/inc contract and validation
body: Implement increment endpoint accepting {delta, op_id}. Enforce payload validation, bounded delta rules, and request tracing metadata.

title: [Feature] Publish increment events to partitioned inc_events topic
body: Gateway publishes normalized increment events to inc_events keyed for partition locality. Include publish metrics and error handling.

title: [Feature] Build worker consume -> aggregate -> Redis flush loop
body: Implement consumer worker that batches increments and flushes updates to Redis. Commit offsets only after successful flush.

title: [Feature] Implement GET /counters/{id} and POST /counters/bulk_get
body: Expose low-latency single-key read endpoint and bounded bulk read endpoint for dashboard and verification workflows.

title: [Epic] Correctness: idempotent processing under retries
body:
Objective: ensure duplicate logical operations do not over-apply during retries and reprocessing.

Acceptance criteria:
- op_id dedupe policy implemented and documented.
- Retry-heavy workload produces zero correctness mismatches within dedupe window.

title: [Feature] Add op_id dedupe store with TTL and memory budget controls
body: Add dedupe guard keyed by op_id with explicit TTL boundary. Document memory growth assumptions and fallback behavior when pressure rises.

title: [Benchmark] Retry-heavy correctness audit with duplicate op_id traffic
body: Run workload with intentional retries and duplicate op_id values. Compare expected totals vs observed totals and record dedupe hit ratio.

title: [Epic] Scale: skewed load and hot-key mitigation
body:
Objective: characterize skew behavior and mitigate hot-key bottlenecks.

Acceptance criteria:
- Baseline skew benchmark without striping is recorded.
- Striping implementation is benchmarked against baseline.
- Improvement claims are evidence-backed.

title: [Feature] Build load generator profiles (uniform, Zipf, burst, retry)
body: Implement configurable load generator with concurrency, key distribution, retry behavior, and burst controls suitable for local and k8s runs.

title: [Benchmark] Baseline run with uniform key distribution
body: Run benchmark under uniform distribution to establish baseline throughput and latency before skew-focused optimizations.

title: [Benchmark] Skew run with Zipf distribution (no striping)
body: Execute skewed workload without hot-key striping. Capture lag concentration, throughput ceiling, and p95/p99 latency impact.

title: [Feature] Implement striped counter mode for hot logical keys
body: Add logical->physical stripe mapping and deterministic stripe routing. Update read path to aggregate stripes safely.

title: [Benchmark] Skew run with striping enabled and compare vs baseline
body: Repeat Zipf skew scenario with striping enabled and produce side-by-side comparison (throughput, p95/p99, lag concentration).

title: [Epic] Operability: backpressure, replay, and failure drills
body:
Objective: keep system stable under stress and prove recoverability.

Acceptance criteria:
- Bounded backpressure and shed signals implemented.
- Replay workflow rebuilds state from log/snapshot.
- Core failure drills are executed and documented.

title: [Feature] Implement bounded ingress queue, adaptive batching, and 429 shedding
body: Add deterministic overload behavior: bounded queue depth, adaptive batch size limits, and explicit request shedding with metrics.

title: [Feature] Implement admin replay endpoint for state rebuild
body: Add replay path to rebuild materialized state from event log and optional snapshots. Track replay duration and correctness checks.

title: [Feature] Add Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboards
body: Instrument gateway and workers for ingest rate, lag, latency, dedupe hit ratio, shed rate, and Redis latency. Ship dashboard definitions.

title: [Drill] Kill worker during peak load and verify convergence
body: Terminate one worker under sustained load. Confirm lag recovery, rebalance behavior, and invariant correctness.

title: [Drill] Force rebalance by scaling worker replicas mid-run
body: Scale worker count during active load to trigger partition reassignment. Validate no prolonged correctness drift.

title: [Drill] Inject Redis latency and validate graceful degradation
body: Inject downstream latency and observe queue depth, shed rate, and service stability. Confirm system avoids collapse.

title: [Drill] Restart broker during load and verify recovery behavior
body: Restart message broker under active traffic. Measure disruption window, recovery, and post-recovery correctness.

title: [Drill] Wipe Redis and rebuild state via replay
body: Delete materialized state and run replay workflow. Verify recovered totals match expected values and report replay duration.

title: [Epic] Technical publication and walkthrough readiness
body:
Objective: package implementation results into technical publication artifacts.

Acceptance criteria:
- Technical summary notes updated with validated metrics.
- Blog case studies published from real evidence.
- Architecture walkthrough script complete.

title: [Feature] Publish case-study article with benchmark and drill evidence
body: Write a long-form technical narrative with architecture choices, footguns, benchmark data, and reliability drill outcomes.

title: [Feature] Finalize technical outcome summary with measured results
body: Update technical outcome summary with hard numbers and defensible claims sourced from benchmark/drill artifacts.

title: [Feature] Build architecture walkthrough script from project docs
body: Prepare a concise walkthrough script covering design tradeoffs, correctness model, scaling strategy, and failure handling.

title: [Feature] Optional: add k8s autoscaling based on consumer lag
body: Add optional KEDA or custom metric autoscaling for worker replicas using lag thresholds and cooldown windows.
